tier_thresholds.description: GREEN < 0.35 <= AMBER < 0.55 <= RED on recalibrated probability

H5_fleet_percentile.definition: >=p85 of fleet same-week scores in >=4 of trailing 6 weeks

A2_battery_cascade.definition: rest-VSI step <= -0.5 V (SNR>=2) AND drive-VSI step >= +0.3 V (SNR>=2) within +-8 wk AND dip widening > +1 V (last 60 d vs earlier, >=10 events each)

A1_crank_burst.definition: daily failed-cranks+retries 7-d rolling sum > own-first-half mean + 3sigma (floor >=3) for >=2 consecutive days

persistence_terminal.definition: weekly vsi-std ratio > training NF p90 envelope >=4 of last 12 wk AND still active at evaluation date (terminal-state flag only)
persistence_terminal.reference: training NF p90 vsi-std ratio envelope

silence_trigger.definition: no telemetry >30 d while AMBER or RED tier

alert_precedence.description: Evidence-state machine — apply in order; first matching rule wins

rules.notes: Highest priority; battery root cause most likely

rules.trigger: RED tier (single week, no higher priority trigger)
rules.action: Queue inspection, rank by H1_momentum then H5 co-fire
rules.notes: Promote to P0 if H2 fires before scheduled visit
Promote if H1 AND H5 both fire

rules.action: Attach evidence to existing alert; do not page
rules.notes: Never generate standalone alert; annotate existing P0/P1/P2 row

rules.trigger: silence_overlay (>30 d no telemetry while AMBER/RED)
rules.notes: Overlays other priorities; does not replace inspection alert

window_matrix.note: Embedded below from failure_window_matrix.csv (retrospective n values only; NOT a countdown clock)

rows.honest_caveat: Retrospective n=4 only. Min lead 28d so 2-week scheduling is tight. NF false alarms: 0/20. NOT a countdown clock.
Leads vs t_end/JCOPENDATE. Long median lead (~months) means condition flag, NOT failure-imminent alarm. 4/20 NF also end in persistence fire state. NOT a countdown clock.
First-fire combined-channel leads for RED-tier failed trucks. 2 RED NF trucks are false positives. Monitoring only; act on channel fire. NOT a countdown clock.
0 failed trucks scored AMBER in OOF validation — no empirical lead-time data. AMBER = borderline risk. 2/20 NF scored AMBER. Treat as uncertain risk zone requiring monitoring.

rows.scheduling_window_d: Next scheduled service (50000 km or 6 months)
rows.honest_caveat: Leads shown are when channel fired, not from GREEN score issuance. 1 GREEN-failed (VIN9_F) fired nothing — irreducible blind spot. NOT a countdown clock.
SMA-dead trucks can still be detected via VSI persistence. VIN9_F was fully blind on all channels. n=2 observed cases; very limited evidence base.

watchlist.note: Pre-registered NF VINs showing anomalous signals; monitor with elevated cadence

SMA_dead.note: SMA telemetry absent or near-zero throughout; A1 channel not applicable; dip_depth_last90_delta set to NaN

refit_gate: D8 gates only — no retuning outside formal D8 evaluation protocol

onboarding.note: Trucks with weeks_observed < min_weeks_mature are marked immature. weeks_observed is OPERATIONAL METADATA for maturity gating only — observation length remains BANNED as a model feature (leak class D5).
